goal: Seal prepare emits a pre-populated SealResult starter so agents patch instead of type from scratch

summary.title: Seal prepare emits a pre-populated SealResult starter
summary.what: Add `Starter *SealResult` to `domain.SealPreparePackage` (omitempty for backward compat). `engine.buildSealStarter` pre-fills the deterministic fields agents shouldn't have to type: intent_id from the draft, fingerprint.files_touched from the diff, fingerprint.subsystems via the existing path-derived helper. Agent-judgment fields (title, what, why, decisions, risks, anti_patterns, confidence) remain empty so the schema is visible and the editing target is obvious. The `instruction` text on the prepare package now points agents at the starter as the recommended starting point.
summary.why: First-touch trial users hit the steepest cliff at `seal --submit`: filling the SealResult JSON by hand from a multi-line schema instruction. ValidateSealResult requires six fields, three of which are derivable from data the engine already has. Pre-filling them removes ~50% of the typing without removing any of the agent-judgment surface.
summary.user_goal: An agent running the seal flow for the first time copies the starter, patches in the agent-judgment fields, and submits — without typing the deterministic shape of the JSON.

decisions.rationale: These three are deterministic from the draft + diff and have no judgment component. The other required fields (title, what, why) are agent-judgment and would over-promise if pre-filled with placeholders that look like real content.
Additive, non-breaking. Older readers parse the package unchanged. The pointer-with-omitempty pattern matches Snapshot, the only other optional field on the package.

decisions.chose: Empty strings + empty arrays, NOT placeholder text like 'TODO: fill in'.
decisions.rationale: Placeholder text would survive into actually-submitted seals if an agent forgot to overwrite it. Empty values force the issue: the validator rejects empty title/what/why with a clear error so the agent learns the contract.

decisions.chose: Reuse subsystemsFromFiles — same helper conflict-detection uses.
decisions.rationale: Seal-time and check-time should agree on what counts as a subsystem; sharing the helper keeps them in sync.

rejected.alternative: Pre-fill title from the draft Goal text.
rejected.reason: Goal is what the agent claimed at start; title is what they accomplished at seal. Conflating them would let agents ship seals where the title doesn't reflect the actual work.

rejected.alternative: Pre-fill what/why from a turns-aggregation heuristic.
rejected.reason: What and why are the agent's load-bearing summary; deriving them from turns smells helpful but produces low-quality boilerplate. Lint would reject most of it as boilerplate_what anyway.

rejected.alternative: Add a --auto-confirm flag that auto-submits the starter as-is.
rejected.reason: Submitting a starter without agent-judgment fields would write a seal that the validator rejects (empty title/what/why). The flag wouldn't help anyone.

risks: Agents that already have a hand-built seal.json template won't pick up the starter automatically — they'll keep using their template until they read the new instruction text. Acceptable: the change is additive; nothing breaks.

Over-pre-filling could lead agents to skip thinking about the fingerprint. Mitigation: subsystems are path-derived (often coarse); agents who care about precision will refine them, agents who don't get a passable default rather than nothing.

anti_patterns.what: Pre-filling agent-judgment fields with placeholder text
anti_patterns.why: Placeholder text survives into actually-submitted seals when agents forget to overwrite. Empty values force the validator to reject so the agent learns.

anti_patterns.what: Auto-submitting the starter without agent-judgment fields
anti_patterns.why: ValidateSealResult would reject (empty what/why). A flag that does this would invite users to think the validator is broken when they get the rejection.

followups: Consider an `--instruction-only` flag on prepare that omits Starter when the agent prefers writing from scratch (some downstream tooling parses Starter as a no-op).
Add a CLI flag `--starter` that prints just the starter JSON (skip the rest of the package) for shell pipelines that pipe directly into `mainline seal --submit`.
Consider auto-deriving Title from a one-line summary of Decisions[0].Chose once the agent has filled them — but only as a hint, not as a default.

architectural_claims: Pre-fill what's deterministic, leave what requires judgment empty — never placeholder-fill agent-judgment fields.
Starter is additive (omitempty *SealResult); older readers parse unchanged.
Seal-time subsystem derivation reuses the same helper conflict-detection uses; both surfaces stay in sync.
behavioral_changes: `mainline seal --prepare` output gains a `seal_result_starter` field carrying intent_id + files_touched + path-derived subsystems.
The `instruction` text on the prepare package points agents at the starter as the recommended starting point.

data_model_changes.name: SealPreparePackage JSON shape
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Additive: existing readers ignore unknown fields. Persisted snapshots written by older binaries are missing the field; that's fine — Starter is optional.
